confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_5c92d2fc-4b31-4aec-8abd-1d93fd4fdee3 read back as promoted

evidence: Blocking defect: EE-MCP cwd allowlist accepted /home/andrey2 because implementation used raw StartsWith against /home/andrey.
Task review command refused --agent narada-andrey.Kevin with Agent not found in roster.
Task review command then refused narada-andrey.architect because architect role is not reviewer/admin.
Review was finally recorded as operator, even though the intended reviewing identity was Kevin acting as architect/reviewer.
proposal: Define CAPA trigger criteria for review findings that reveal authority-boundary defects in execution surfaces, especially MCP/EE-MCP, secret handling, routing, and cross-embodiment execution.
Require reviewer identity/role mismatch during governed review to create a CAPA or at least an incident envelope before continuing through workaround identities.
Add lifecycle support for declared reviewer identities or explicit architect-as-reviewer authority when a Site uses named architect identities such as narada-andrey.Kevin.
Add review command guidance: if requested reviewer cannot be used due to roster/role mismatch, report exact repair path and suggest CAPA classification.
payload.recommendation: Treat as CAPA for review authority coherence and execution-boundary review handling. Create follow-up tasks for reviewer roster semantics and CAPA trigger guidance.
payload.summary: During doctrine-alignment review of narada-andrey task 62, Kevin found a blocking EE-MCP cwd boundary defect and a lifecycle-role incoherence: narada-andrey.Kevin was requested as reviewer but task review refused Kevin because the lifecycle roster did not admit him as reviewer/admin. The review was recorded as operator, which preserved task state but exposed an authority/identity mismatch.
payload.title: CAPA: Task 62 review found authority-boundary defect and reviewer roster incoherence
